== Notes for [[Item:Q102878|Q102878]] ==

* Wikipedia: [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moses_ben_Hanoch Moses ben Hanoch] & [https://he.wikipedia.org/wiki/%D7%9E%D7%A9%D7%94_%D7%91%D7%9F_%D7%97%D7%A0%D7%95%D7%9A משה בן חנוך.]
* The Jewish Encyclopedia: [https://www.jewishencyclopedia.com/articles/11078-moses-ben-enoch Moses ben Enoch.]
* About the kidnapping: [https://he.wikipedia.org/wiki/%D7%90%D7%A8%D7%91%D7%A2%D7%AA_%D7%94%D7%A9%D7%91%D7%95%D7%99%D7%99%D7%9D מעשה ארבעת השבויים]
* Primary source about him: Sefer ha-Ḳabbalah [http://www.daat.ac.il/daat/vl/ravad/ravad01.pdf ספר הקבלה].

Founder of Talmud study in Cordoba, Spain.

Moses was one of the four scholars who went from Sura, the seat of a once flourishing but then declining Talmudic academy, in order to collect contributions for that school. During a voyage from Bari, on the coast of Italy, they were captured by the Moorish-Spanish admiral Ibn Rumahis, Moses was taken to Cordova.

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
geni:6000000034590754631

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]]
[[Category:Possibly derived from wikipedia]][[Category:Pages]]
